November 2019 - Lake Jackson District approved new Safe Welding Area. The rig underwent a mooring upgrade; therefore, the deck configuration changed and Seadrill submitted for approval a revised deck layout schematic. The location of the SWA is the same; the drawing was only revised to include the addition of anchor handling winches.
The previous comment from 2015 was addressed, and a new approval was granted by New Orleans District June 2018.
Rig does not have an approved safe welding area any longer.  The approved SWA was revoked due to Mud Pit vents too close to the SWA in December 2015 during inspection.

It was found during inspection on 1/3/13 that the accumulator system has a High Pressure (HP) Shear function on the main station but not at the remote station. When the HP Shear mode is needed to shear the pipe in use, the rig must conduct operations in HP mode, but may conduct tests and functions in normal mode.
Rig does not have a Safe Burn Area approved.  Rig utilizes Hot Work Permits only.
